title: Morphysics
body:
# SkiaSharp + .NET MAUI Procedural Morphing + Deterministic Physics Micro-Engine
## Complete Implementation Specification for GitHub Copilot Agent

---

## 🎯 MISSION CRITICAL: This Implementation Must Succeed

You are implementing a **production-quality SkiaSharp-powered micro-engine** for .NET MAUI. This is not a prototype or proof-of-concept. Every component must be:
- ✅ Fully functional
- ✅ Properly tested
- ✅ Performance-validated
- ✅ Appium-verified
- ✅ Production-ready

**⚠️ FAILURE IS NOT AN OPTION** - Use the checklists and validation criteria throughout to ensure completeness.

---

## 📋 PROJECT CONTEXT

**Target Framework:** .NET 10 + C# 14  
**Namespace:** `SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui.Morphysics`  
**Primary Dependencies:** SkiaSharp, SkiaSharp.Views.Maui  
**Integration Point:** Existing MAUI application (do not restructure repo)  
**Asset Loading:** MAUI Resources by filename only  
**Testing:** Existing Appium infrastructure (do not modify)

---

## 🎯 PRIMARY OBJECTIVE

Create a reusable MAUI library that developers can use from XAML + MVVM to build interactive, animated, physics-driven scenes.

### Core Capabilities Required
- ✅ Runtime vector morphing between SVG path strings
- ✅ Deterministic physics engine (fixed timestep + seeded RNG)
- ✅ Particle emitter system with collisions, attractors, sticky zones
- ✅ Sprite rendering (single image or sprite sheet)
- ✅ Retained scene graph with named nodes
- ✅ Property animation timelines
- ✅ GPU-accelerated rendering (SKGLView)
- ✅ XAML/MVVM-friendly API
- ✅ Appium-validated UI behavior
- ✅ Comprehensive test coverage

---

## 📐 IMPLEMENTATION PHASES WITH SUCCESS CRITERIA

### PHASE 1: Foundation & Core API (MUST COMPLETE FIRST)
**Duration Target:** Days 1-2

#### 1.1 Public API Implementation
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] `AnimatedCanvasView` class created with all required members
- [ ] `SceneNode` abstract base class implemented
- [ ] `VectorNode` with morph support implemented
- [ ] `ParticleEmitter` class implemented
- [ ] `Particle` class implemented
- [ ] `PhysicsWorld` class implemented
- [ ] `Timeline` class implemented
- [ ] `MorphTarget` class implemented
- [ ] `RenderContext` helper class created
- [ ] All public APIs compile without errors
- [ ] XML documentation added to all public members

**Validation Criteria:**
```csharp
// This code MUST compile and run without errors:
var canvas = new AnimatedCanvasView();
canvas.SetDeterministicSeed(42);
var node = new VectorNode { Id = "test", PathData = "M 0,0 L 100,100" };
canvas.Root = node;
var timeline = new Timeline()
    .Target(node)
    .Animate("X", 0f, 100f, 1000)
    .Then(500)
    .Call(() => Debug.WriteLine("Complete"));
canvas.PlayTimeline(timeline);
```

#### 1.2 Rendering Infrastructure
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] SKGLView integration working
- [ ] SKCanvasView fallback implemented
- [ ] Scene graph traversal algorithm implemented
- [ ] Transform hierarchy (parent-child) working correctly
- [ ] Paint/brush system functional
- [ ] Frame timing mechanism implemented
- [ ] Canvas ready event firing correctly

**Validation Criteria:**
- Can render a single colored rectangle at 60 FPS
- Can render nested scene nodes with correct transforms
- Canvas dimensions reported correctly
- Paint colors applied correctly

---

### PHASE 2: Vector Morphing System
**Duration Target:** Days 2-3

#### 2.1 SVG Path Parsing
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] SVG path string parser implemented
- [ ] Support for M, L, C, Q, A commands
- [ ] Path normalization to consistent format
- [ ] Path to point array conversion
- [ ] Point count alignment algorithm implemented
- [ ] Unit tests for all SVG commands pass

**Validation Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public void ParseSvgPath_CircleToSquare_CorrectPointCount()
{
    var circle = "M 50,0 A 50,50 0 1,1 50,100 A 50,50 0 1,1 50,0 Z";
    var square = "M 0,0 L 100,0 L 100,100 L 0,100 Z";
    var morpher = new PathMorpher(circle, square);
    Assert.That(morpher.SourcePoints.Length, Is.EqualTo(morpher.TargetPoints.Length));
}
```

#### 2.2 Morphing Interpolation
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Per-vertex linear interpolation working
- [ ] Easing functions integrated (Linear, EaseIn, EaseOut, EaseInOut)
- [ ] Progress value (0.0 to 1.0) correctly interpolates
- [ ] Multiple morph targets supported
- [ ] Morph target switching smooth
- [ ] Real-time morphing renders at 60 FPS

**Validation Criteria:**
- Morphing from circle to square shows smooth transition
- MorphProgress = 0.5 shows exactly halfway between shapes
- No visual glitches or jumps during morph
- Performance: Can morph 5 paths simultaneously at 60 FPS

---

### PHASE 3: Deterministic Physics Engine
**Duration Target:** Days 3-5

#### 3.1 Core Physics Loop
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Fixed timestep integration (1/60s default)
- [ ] Velocity Verlet or RK4 integrator implemented
- [ ] Seeded random number generator (System.Random with seed)
- [ ] Gravity application working
- [ ] Particle position/velocity updates deterministic
- [ ] Physics step accumulator prevents spiral of death

**Validation Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public void Physics_DeterministicReplay_IdenticalResults()
{
    var world1 = new PhysicsWorld();
    world1.SetSeed(12345);
    var p1 = new Particle { Position = Vector2.Zero, Velocity = new Vector2(10, 0) };
    
    var world2 = new PhysicsWorld();
    world2.SetSeed(12345);
    var p2 = new Particle { Position = Vector2.Zero, Velocity = new Vector2(10, 0) };
    
    for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++)
    {
        world1.Step(1/60f);
        world2.Step(1/60f);
    }
    
    Assert.That(p1.Position, Is.EqualTo(p2.Position).Within(0.0001f));
}
```

#### 3.2 Collision Detection
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Circle-circle collision detection
- [ ] AABB collision detection (optional)
- [ ] Particle-particle collisions
- [ ] Particle-scene node collisions
- [ ] Collision response (bounce) working
- [ ] Collision callbacks firing

**Validation Criteria:**
- Two particles collide and bounce apart
- Collision detection runs in O(n²) or better with spatial partitioning
- No tunneling through objects at high velocities

#### 3.3 Attractors & Sticky Zones
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Attractor force calculation (inverse square law)
- [ ] Multiple attractors supported
- [ ] Attractor strength configurable
- [ ] Sticky zones capture particles probabilistically
- [ ] Stuck particles stop moving
- [ ] Attractors can be attached to scene nodes

**Validation Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public void Attractor_PullsParticle_TowardTarget()
{
    var world = new PhysicsWorld();
    var target = new VectorNode { X = 100, Y = 100 };
    world.AddAttractor("test", target, strength: 100f);
    
    var particle = new Particle { Position = Vector2.Zero };
    world.AddParticle(particle);
    
    world.Step(1/60f);
    
    // Particle should move toward (100, 100)
    Assert.That(particle.Velocity.X, Is.GreaterThan(0));
    Assert.That(particle.Velocity.Y, Is.GreaterThan(0));
}
```

---

### PHASE 4: Particle System
**Duration Target:** Days 5-6

#### 4.1 Particle Emitter
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Emission rate (particles/second) working
- [ ] Burst emission (spawn N immediately) working
- [ ] Particle lifetime countdown and removal
- [ ] Max particle cap enforced
- [ ] Particle pooling for performance (reuse objects)
- [ ] Initial velocity/direction configurable
- [ ] Spawn position variance

**Validation Criteria:**
- Rate = 60 spawns exactly 60 particles/second
- EmitBurst(100) creates 100 particles immediately
- Dead particles are removed from simulation
- MaxParticles = 500 prevents exceeding limit

#### 4.2 Sprite Rendering
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Single sprite image loading from MAUI Resources
- [ ] Sprite sheet support (grid-based frames)
- [ ] Frame extraction by index
- [ ] Sprite batching for performance
- [ ] Sprite rotation/scale working
- [ ] Alpha/tint support

**Validation Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public void SpriteSheet_ExtractsCorrectFrame()
{
    var emitter = new ParticleEmitter
    {
        SpriteFilename = "particles.png",
        UseSpriteSheet = true,
        FrameWidth = 32,
        FrameHeight = 32
    };
    
    var frame = emitter.GetFrame(5); // Frame index 5
    Assert.That(frame.Width, Is.EqualTo(32));
    Assert.That(frame.Height, Is.EqualTo(32));
}
```

---

### PHASE 5: Timeline Animation System
**Duration Target:** Days 6-7

#### 5.1 Property Animation
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Any BindableProperty can be animated
- [ ] Float, Color, Vector2 property types supported
- [ ] From/To value interpolation working
- [ ] Duration in milliseconds respected
- [ ] Easing functions applied correctly
- [ ] Multiple properties can animate simultaneously

**Validation Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task Timeline_AnimatesProperty_OverDuration()
{
    var node = new VectorNode { X = 0 };
    var timeline = new Timeline()
        .Target(node)
        .Animate("X", 0f, 100f, 1000);
    
    timeline.Play();
    await Task.Delay(500); // Halfway
    
    Assert.That(node.X, Is.EqualTo(50f).Within(5f));
}
```

#### 5.2 Sequencing & Callbacks
**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Then(delayMs) adds pause between animations
- [ ] Call(callback) executes action at right time
- [ ] Timelines can chain multiple animations
- [ ] Timelines can loop/repeat
- [ ] Timeline state (playing/paused/stopped) tracked
- [ ] Multiple timelines run concurrently

---

### PHASE 6: Asset Loading & Resource Management
**Duration Target:** Days 7-8

**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] Images load from MAUI Resources/Images folder
- [ ] Filename-only references work (no path needed)
- [ ] Image caching implemented (don't reload)
- [ ] Null/missing image handling (error logging)
- [ ] SKBitmap disposal on cleanup
- [ ] Memory usage stays stable during asset loading

**Validation Criteria:**
```csharp
// This MUST work without full path:
var emitter = new ParticleEmitter { SpriteFilename = "particle.png" };
// System finds it in Resources/Images/particle.png automatically
```

---

### PHASE 7: XAML Integration & Data Binding
**Duration Target:** Days 8-9

**Status Checklist:**
- [ ] AnimatedCanvasView usable in XAML
- [ ] Root property supports XAML declaration
- [ ] Scene nodes have x:Name support
- [ ] Properties support {Binding} expressions
- [ ] INotifyPropertyChanged implemented correctly
- [ ] Design-time rendering supported (no crashes)

**Validation Test:**
```xml
<!-- This XAML MUST work: -->
<skia:AnimatedCanvasView>
    <skia:AnimatedCanvasView.Root>
        <skia:VectorNode Id="Logo" 
                         PathData="{Binding LogoPath}"
                         X="{Binding LogoX}"
                         Y="{Binding LogoY}" />
    </skia:AnimatedCanvasView.Root>
</skia:AnimatedCanvasView>
```

---

## 🧪 TESTING REQUIREMENTS (MANDATORY)

### Unit Test Coverage Requirements
**Minimum 85% code coverage for:**
- [ ] Physics integrator
- [ ] SVG path parser
- [ ] Morph interpolation
- [ ] Timeline sequencing
- [ ] Collision detection
- [ ] Attractor forces
- [ ] Particle lifecycle
- [ ] Scene graph operations

**Test Organization:**
```
Tests/
  ├── Unit/
  │   ├── Physics/
  │   │   ├── PhysicsWorldTests.cs
  │   │   ├── CollisionTests.cs
  │   │   └── AttractorTests.cs
  │   ├── Morphing/
  │   │   ├── PathParserTests.cs
  │   │   └── MorphInterpolationTests.cs
  │   ├── Particles/
  │   │   └── ParticleEmitterTests.cs
  │   └── Animations/
  │       └── TimelineTests.cs
  ├── Integration/
  │   ├── XamlBindingTests.cs
  │   ├── SceneRenderingTests.cs
  │   └── PhysicsTimelineTests.cs
  └── UI/
      └── Appium/
          ├── Sample01_MarblesTests.cs
          ├── Sample02_DataParticlesTests.cs
          └── ... (one per sample)
```

### Integration Test Checklist
- [ ] XAML binding works end-to-end
- [ ] ViewModel property changes update scene
- [ ] Timeline + physics interaction works
- [ ] Multi-node scene renders correctly
- [ ] Memory usage stable over 60 seconds
- [ ] No crashes under typical usage

---

## 📱 APPIUM UI TEST SPECIFICATION

### Test Pattern (MUST USE FOR ALL SAMPLES)
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task Sample01_Marbles_RenderAndInteract()
{
    // 1. SETUP: Launch sample page
    driver.Navigate().GoToPage("Sample01_Marbles");
    
    // 2. WAIT: Ensure canvas ready
    await WaitForCanvasReady(timeout: 5000);
    
    // 3. BASELINE: Capture initial state
    var screenshot1 = await CaptureScreenshot("marbles_initial");
    
    // 4. INTERACT: Perform user action (tilt device simulation)
    await SimulateTilt(x: 0.5f, y: 0.5f);
    await Task.Delay(1000); // Wait for physics response
    
    // 5. VALIDATE: Capture after interaction
    var screenshot2 = await CaptureScreenshot("marbles_tilted");
    
    // 6. ASSERTIONS: Visual validation
    var diff = CompareScreenshots(screenshot1, screenshot2);
    Assert.That(diff.PercentChanged, Is.GreaterThan(5), 
        "Scene should change after tilt");
    
    // 7. DETERMINISM: Export and validate state
    var debugState = await GetDebugState();
    Assert.That(debugState.ParticleCount, Is.GreaterThan(0));
    Assert.That(debugState.FPS, Is.GreaterThan(50));
    
    // 8. REPLAY: Reset with same seed, verify identical result
    await ResetWithSeed(12345);
    await Task.Delay(1000);
    var replayState = await GetDebugState();
    Assert.That(replayState.Checksum, Is.EqualTo(debugState.Checksum),
        "Deterministic replay failed");
}
```

### Debug Endpoints Required
Implement these methods in `AnimatedCanvasView`:
```csharp
public async Task<DebugState> GetDebugStateAsync()
{
    return new DebugState
    {
        FPS = currentFPS,
        ParticleCount = physics.ActiveParticles.Count,
        NodeCount = CountNodes(Root),
        Checksum = ComputeStateChecksum(), // For determinism validation
        MemoryUsageMB = GC.GetTotalMemory(false) / 1024 / 1024
    };
}
```

### Appium Test Checklist (Per Sample)
- [ ] Test launches sample page successfully
- [ ] Test waits for canvas ready event
- [ ] Test captures initial screenshot
- [ ] Test performs meaningful interaction
- [ ] Test captures post-interaction screenshot
- [ ] Test validates visual changes occurred
- [ ] Test validates FPS ≥ 50
- [ ] Test validates deterministic replay
- [ ] Test validates memory usage < 100MB
- [ ] Test passes consistently (3/3 runs)

---

## 🎨 SAMPLE GALLERY IMPLEMENTATION

### Implementation Checklist (Per Sample)
Each sample MUST have:
- [ ] XAML page (e.g., `Sample01_Marbles.xaml`)
- [ ] ViewModel inheriting from `BaseViewModel`
- [ ] Proper MVVM separation (no code-behind logic)
- [ ] At least one interactive element (button, slider, gesture)
- [ ] Appium UI test (pattern above)
- [ ] Performance test recording FPS and memory
- [ ] README with description and usage
- [ ] Screenshots of expected output

---

### Sample 01: Animated Marbles Background
**File:** `Samples/Sample01_Marbles/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] 50-100 marble particles rendered as sprites
- [ ] Marbles roll and collide with each other
- [ ] Device accelerometer tilts gravity direction
- [ ] Marbles bounce off screen edges
- [ ] Smooth 60 FPS performance
- [ ] Deterministic physics (can replay)

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task Marbles_TiltDevice_MarblesRoll()
{
    // Tilt device right → marbles roll right
    await SimulateAccelerometer(x: 1.0f, y: 0f);
    await Task.Delay(2000);
    
    var state = await GetDebugState();
    var avgX = state.Particles.Average(p => p.Position.X);
    
    Assert.That(avgX, Is.GreaterThan(screenWidth / 2),
        "Marbles should accumulate on right side");
}
```

**ViewModel Properties:**
```csharp
public class Sample01ViewModel : BaseViewModel
{
    public int MarbleCount { get; set; } = 75;
    public float Gravity { get; set; } = 9.8f;
    public float Bounciness { get; set; } = 0.7f;
    public string MarbleSprite { get; set; } = "marble.png";
}
```

---

### Sample 02: Data-Driven Particles Clustering
**File:** `Samples/Sample02_DataClustering/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Load mock dataset (e.g., regional statistics)
- [ ] Each data point = particle with metadata
- [ ] Particles cluster around morphing region shapes
- [ ] Shape morphs from map → bar chart → pie chart
- [ ] Sticky zones capture particles in regions
- [ ] Data tooltip on particle tap

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task DataClustering_MorphShape_ParticlesFollow()
{
    // Start: particles around map shape
    var initialCentroid = await GetParticleCentroid();
    
    // Morph to bar chart shape
    await TapButton("MorphToBar");
    await Task.Delay(2000);
    
    var finalCentroid = await GetParticleCentroid();
    
    Assert.That(finalCentroid, Is.Not.EqualTo(initialCentroid),
        "Particles should move with morphing shape");
}
```

**ViewModel:**
```csharp
public class Sample02ViewModel : BaseViewModel
{
    public ObservableCollection<DataPoint> DataPoints { get; set; }
    public string CurrentShape { get; set; } = "map";
    public float ClusterStrength { get; set; } = 50f;
    
    public ICommand MorphToBarCommand { get; }
    public ICommand MorphToPieCommand { get; }
}
```

---

### Sample 03: Interactive Brand Logo Morph
**File:** `Samples/Sample03_BrandLogoMorph/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Two logo SVG paths loaded
- [ ] Button morphs between Logo A ↔ Logo B
- [ ] Particle trails emit from morphing edges
- [ ] Trail particles fade out over lifetime
- [ ] Smooth 1-second morph animation
- [ ] Tap logo to trigger burst of particles

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task LogoMorph_EmitsTrails()
{
    await TapButton("Morph");
    await Task.Delay(500); // Mid-morph
    
    var state = await GetDebugState();
    Assert.That(state.ParticleCount, Is.GreaterThan(20),
        "Should emit trail particles during morph");
}
```

**ViewModel:**
```csharp
public class Sample03ViewModel : BaseViewModel
{
    public string LogoPathA { get; set; } = "M 0,0 L 100,0 L 50,100 Z";
    public string LogoPathB { get; set; } = "M 25,25 L 75,25 L 75,75 L 25,75 Z";
    public float MorphProgress { get; set; }
    public int TrailParticlesPerFrame { get; set; } = 5;
    
    public ICommand MorphCommand { get; }
}
```

---

### Sample 04: Gamified Micro-Interactions
**File:** `Samples/Sample04_Gamification/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Toggle button with rewarding feedback
- [ ] Button press spawns confetti particles
- [ ] Reward ring morphs and fills
- [ ] Sound effect plays (optional)
- [ ] Haptic feedback (optional)
- [ ] Particles clear after 2 seconds

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task Toggle_SpawnsConfetti()
{
    var initialCount = await GetParticleCount();
    
    await TapButton("RewardToggle");
    await Task.Delay(100);
    
    var afterCount = await GetParticleCount();
    Assert.That(afterCount, Is.GreaterThan(initialCount + 50),
        "Should spawn confetti burst");
}
```

---

### Sample 05: Educational Physics Playground
**File:** `Samples/Sample05_PhysicsPlayground/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Sliders for gravity, force strength, particle count
- [ ] Button to spawn particle burst
- [ ] Draggable attractors
- [ ] Editable morph target paths (text input)
- [ ] Real-time FPS and particle count display
- [ ] Reset button clears scene

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task Playground_ChangeGravity_AffectsParticles()
{
    await SetSliderValue("GravitySlider", 0); // Zero gravity
    await SpawnParticles(10);
    await Task.Delay(1000);
    
    var state1 = await GetDebugState();
    var avgVelocity1 = state1.Particles.Average(p => p.Velocity.Length());
    
    await SetSliderValue("GravitySlider", 100); // High gravity
    await Task.Delay(1000);
    
    var state2 = await GetDebugState();
    var avgVelocity2 = state2.Particles.Average(p => p.Velocity.Length());
    
    Assert.That(avgVelocity2, Is.GreaterThan(avgVelocity1));
}
```

---

### Sample 06: AR-Style Overlay
**File:** `Samples/Sample06_AROverlay/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Mock camera feed background (static image in tests)
- [ ] Silhouette path detected from "camera" (mock detector)
- [ ] Particles attracted to silhouette edges
- [ ] Particles glow/highlight detected regions
- [ ] Path updates every 100ms (mock tracking)
- [ ] Works in CI without real camera

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task AROverlay_ParticlesAttractToSilhouette()
{
    // Mock detector returns person silhouette path
    await StartMockDetection("person_silhouette.svg");
    await Task.Delay(2000);
    
    var state = await GetDebugState();
    var particlesNearSilhouette = CountParticlesNearPath(
        state.Particles, 
        state.AttractorPaths[0], 
        threshold: 20f
    );
    
    Assert.That(particlesNearSilhouette, Is.GreaterThan(state.ParticleCount * 0.5f),
        "Majority of particles should cluster near silhouette");
}
```

---

### Sample 07: Onboarding Morph Reveal
**File:** `Samples/Sample07_OnboardingReveal/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] 3-step onboarding flow
- [ ] Each step: shape morphs to reveal icon/illustration
- [ ] Particles highlight "next" button
- [ ] Progress indicator ring fills
- [ ] "Skip" button available
- [ ] State persists across app restarts

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task Onboarding_AdvanceStep_MorphsToNextIcon()
{
    var shape1 = await GetCurrentMorphPath();
    
    await TapButton("Next");
    await Task.Delay(1500); // Morph duration
    
    var shape2 = await GetCurrentMorphPath();
    
    Assert.That(shape1, Is.Not.EqualTo(shape2),
        "Shape should morph to next step icon");
}
```

---

### Sample 08: Narrative Scene Nodes
**File:** `Samples/Sample08_NarrativeScene/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Story with 5 beats (scenes)
- [ ] Each beat: node morphs + particle effect
- [ ] Timeline orchestrates beat transitions
- [ ] Text overlay shows narration
- [ ] Auto-advance after 3 seconds per beat
- [ ] Scrub bar to jump to any beat

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task NarrativeScene_PlaysFullStory()
{
    await TapButton("PlayStory");
    
    for (int beat = 1; beat <= 5; beat++)
    {
        await WaitForBeat(beat, timeout: 5000);
        var screenshot = await CaptureScreenshot($"beat_{beat}");
        Assert.That(screenshot, Is.Not.Null);
    }
    
    var state = await GetDebugState();
    Assert.That(state.CurrentBeat, Is.EqualTo(5));
}
```

---

### Sample 09: Status Visualizer Ring
**File:** `Samples/Sample09_StatusRing/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Ring shape morphs (loose → tight) based on metric
- [ ] Particles attracted to ring as metric increases
- [ ] Color transitions (red → yellow → green)
- [ ] Metric bound to slider (0-100)
- [ ] Smooth animation (no jumps)
- [ ] Real-time updates (e.g., simulated server data)

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task StatusRing_IncreaseMetric_RingTightens()
{
    await SetSliderValue("MetricSlider", 0);
    var radius1 = await GetRingRadius();
    
    await SetSliderValue("MetricSlider", 100);
    await Task.Delay(1000); // Animation time
    
    var radius2 = await GetRingRadius();
    
    Assert.That(radius2, Is.LessThan(radius1),
        "Ring should tighten as metric increases");
}
```

---

### Sample 10: Widget/Watch Face Effect
**File:** `Samples/Sample10_WidgetEffect/`

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Minimal scene (low battery usage)
- [ ] Short particle bursts only (not continuous)
- [ ] Ring morphs to show time/progress
- [ ] Updates once per second (not 60fps loop)
- [ ] Idle mode: no rendering when unchanged
- [ ] Measure: < 2% CPU, < 20MB memory

**Acceptance Test:**
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task WidgetEffect_LowPowerMode()
{
    await EnableLowPowerMode();
    await Task.Delay(10000); // Run 10 seconds
    
    var metrics = await GetPerformanceMetrics();
    
    Assert.That(metrics.AvgCpuPercent, Is.LessThan(5),
        "CPU usage must be minimal");
    Assert.That(metrics.MemoryMB, Is.LessThan(30),
        "Memory usage must be minimal");
    Assert.That(metrics.AvgFPS, Is.LessThan(10),
        "Should not render continuously");
}
```

---

## ⚡ PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS

### Target Specifications
| Metric | Requirement |
|--------|-------------|
| FPS (Mid-range device) | ≥ 60 FPS with 500 particles + 1 morph |
| Frame time budget | ≤ 12 ms (render + physics) |
| Memory usage | < 100 MB for typical sample |
| GC pressure | < 1 MB/sec allocation rate |
| Startup time | < 2 seconds to first frame |
| Interaction latency | < 100 ms response to touch |

### Performance Test Template
```csharp
[Test]
public async Task PerformanceTest_Sample01_Marbles()
{
    var metrics = new PerformanceMetrics();
    
    // Warm up
    await Task.Delay(2000);
    
    // Measure 30 seconds
    using (metrics.StartRecording())
    {
        await Task.Delay(30000);
    }
    
    Assert.That(metrics.AvgFPS, Is.GreaterThanOrEqualTo(55),
        $"Avg FPS: {metrics.AvgFPS:F1}");
    Assert.That(metrics.P99FrameTime, Is.LessThanOrEqualTo(20),
        $"P99 frame time: {metrics.P99FrameTime:F1}ms");
    Assert.That(metrics.MemoryDeltaMB, Is.LessThan(50),
        $"Memory delta: {metrics.MemoryDeltaMB:F1}MB");
}
```

### Performance Optimization Checklist
- [ ] Object pooling for particles (no new allocations in hot path)
- [ ] Sprite batching (one draw call per sprite sheet)
- [ ] Spatial partitioning for collision detection (grid or quadtree)
- [ ] Dirty rectangle tracking (only redraw changed regions)
- [ ] Aggressive inlining for tight loops
- [ ] SIMD vectorization where applicable (System.Numerics)
- [ ] Avoid LINQ in per-frame code
- [ ] Pre-allocate collections with capacity
- [ ] Cache SKPaint objects (don't recreate)
- [ ] Profile with dotTrace or PerfView

## 🧩 REQUIRED PUBLIC API (EXACT IMPLEMENTATION)

### AnimatedCanvasView
```csharp
namespace SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui.Morphysics;

/// <summary>
/// GPU-accelerated canvas for rendering interactive scenes with physics and morphing.
/// </summary>
public sealed class AnimatedCanvasView : SKGLView
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Root node of the scene graph. All renderable content is a child of this node.
    /// </summary>
    public SceneNode Root { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Collection of animation timelines that can be played on scene nodes.
    /// </summary>
    public AnimationTimelineCollection Animations { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Physics simulation world for particle dynamics.
    /// </summary>
    public PhysicsWorld Physics { get; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Sets the random seed for deterministic physics replay.
    /// </summary>
    /// <param name="seed">Random seed value</param>
    public void SetDeterministicSeed(int seed);

    /// <summary>
    /// Plays the specified animation timeline.
    /// </summary>
    public void PlayTimeline(Timeline timeline);

    /// <summary>
    /// Finds a scene node by its string ID.
    /// </summary>
    /// <typeparam name="T">Type of scene node</typeparam>
    /// <param name="id">Node identifier</param>
    /// <returns>Node instance or null if not found</returns>
    public T? FindNodeById<T>(string id) where T : SceneNode;

    /// <summary>
    /// Captures the current canvas as a PNG screenshot.
    /// </summary>
    public Task<byte[]> CaptureScreenshotAsync(CancellationToken ct = default);

    /// <summary>
    /// Exports the current simulation state as JSON for debugging/replay.
    /// </summary>
    public string ExportDebugStateJson();

    /// <summary>
    /// Fired when the canvas is ready for rendering.
    /// </summary>
    public event EventHandler CanvasReady;
}
```

### Scene Graph
```csharp
namespace SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui.Morphysics;

/// <summary>
/// Base class for all scene nodes that can be rendered and transformed.
/// </summary>
public abstract class SceneNode : BindableObject
{
    public static readonly BindableProperty IdProperty = 
        BindableProperty.Create(nameof(Id), typeof(string), typeof(SceneNode));
    
    public static readonly BindableProperty XProperty = 
        BindableProperty.Create(nameof(X), typeof(float), typeof(SceneNode), 0f);
    
    public static readonly BindableProperty YProperty = 
        BindableProperty.Create(nameof(Y), typeof(float), typeof(SceneNode), 0f);
    
    public static readonly BindableProperty RotationProperty = 
        BindableProperty.Create(nameof(Rotation), typeof(float), typeof(SceneNode), 0f);
    
    public static readonly BindableProperty ScaleProperty = 
        BindableProperty.Create(nameof(Scale), typeof(float), typeof(SceneNode), 1f);

    /// <summary>
    /// Unique identifier for this node (used by FindNodeById).
    /// </summary>
    public string Id
    {
        get => (string)GetValue(IdProperty);
        set => SetValue(IdProperty, value);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// X position in canvas coordinates.
    /// </summary>
    public float X
    {
        get => (float)GetValue(XProperty);
        set => SetValue(XProperty, value);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Y position in canvas coordinates.
    /// </summary>
    public float Y
    {
        get => (float)GetValue(YProperty);
        set => SetValue(YProperty, value);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Rotation in degrees.
    /// </summary>
    public float Rotation
    {
        get => (float)GetValue(RotationProperty);
        set => SetValue(RotationProperty, value);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Uniform scale factor.
    /// </summary>
    public float Scale
    {
        get => (float)GetValue(ScaleProperty);
        set => SetValue(ScaleProperty, value);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Child nodes in the scene graph hierarchy.
    /// </summary>
    public IList<SceneNode> Children { get; } = new List<SceneNode>();

    /// <summary>
    /// Override to implement custom rendering.
    /// </summary>
    public virtual void Draw(SKCanvas canvas, RenderContext ctx) { }
}

/// <summary>
/// Context passed during rendering containing frame info.
/// </summary>
public sealed class RenderContext
{
    public float DeltaTime { get; init; }
    public float TotalTime { get; init; }
    public SKImageInfo ImageInfo { get; init; }
}
```

### VectorNode (Morphing)
```csharp
namespace SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui.Morphysics;

/// <summary>
/// Scene node that renders and morphs between vector paths.
/// </summary>
public sealed class VectorNode : SceneNode
{
    public static readonly BindableProperty PathDataProperty = 
        BindableProperty.Create(nameof(PathData), typeof(string), typeof(VectorNode));
    
    public static readonly BindableProperty MorphProgressProperty = 
        BindableProperty.Create(nameof(MorphProgress), typeof(float), typeof(VectorNode), 0f);

    /// <summary>
    /// SVG path string for the current/base shape.
    /// </summary>
    public string PathData
    {
        get => (string)GetValue(PathDataProperty);
        set => SetValue(PathDataProperty, value);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Collection of target shapes to morph toward.
    /// </summary>
    public IList<MorphTarget> MorphTargets { get; } = new List<MorphTarget>();

    /// <summary>
    /// Morph progress (0.0 = base shape, 1.0 = target shape).
    /// </summary>
    public float MorphProgress
    {
        get => (float)GetValue(MorphProgressProperty);
        set => SetValue(MorphProgressProperty, value);
    }

    /// <summary>
    /// Paint settings for stroke/fill.
    /// </summary>
    public SKPaint Paint { get; set; }
}

/// <summary>
/// Target shape for morphing.
/// </summary>
public sealed class MorphTarget
{
    public string Id { get; set; }
    public string PathData { get; set; }
}
```

### ParticleEmitter
```csharp
namespace SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui.Morphysics;

/// <summary>
/// Scene node that emits particles with physics simulation.
/// </summary>
public sealed class ParticleEmitter : SceneNode
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Particles emitted per second.
    /// </summary>
    public float Rate { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Sprite filename (from MAUI Resources/Images).
    /// </summary>
    public string SpriteFilename { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Whether sprite is a sprite sheet (grid of frames).
    /// </summary>
    public bool UseSpriteSheet { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Width of each frame in sprite sheet (pixels).
    /// </summary>
    public int FrameWidth { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Height of each frame in sprite sheet (pixels).
    /// </summary>
    public int FrameHeight { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Particle lifetime in seconds.
    /// </summary>
    public float ParticleLifetime { get; set; } = 3f;

    /// <summary>
    /// Whether particles participate in physics simulation.
    /// </summary>
    public bool PhysicsEnabled { get; set; } = true;

    /// <summary>
    /// Maximum number of live particles (cap for performance).
    /// </summary>
    public int MaxParticles { get; set; } = 1000;

    /// <summary>
    /// Emits a burst of N particles immediately.
    /// </summary>
    public void EmitBurst(int count);

    /// <summary>
    /// Spawns a single particle at specified position.
    /// </summary>
    public Particle SpawnAt(float x, float y);
}
```

### Particle & Physics
```csharp
namespace SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui.Morphysics;

/// <summary>
/// Single particle with position, velocity, and lifetime.
/// </summary>
public sealed class Particle
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Unique particle ID (for tracking).
    /// </summary>
    public int Id { get; internal set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Current position in world space.
    /// </summary>
    public Vector2 Position { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Current velocity (pixels/second).
    /// </summary>
    public Vector2 Velocity { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Remaining lifetime (seconds).
    /// </summary>
    public float Lifetime { get; set; }

    /// <summary>
    /// Custom metadata (e.g., data point values, colors).
    /// </summary>
    public IDictionary<string, object> Metadata { get; } = new Dictionary<string, object>();
}

/// <summary>
/// Physics simulation for particles.
/// </summary>
public sealed class PhysicsWorld
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Global gravity vector (pixels/second²).
    /// </summary>
    public Vector2 Gravity { get; set; } = new Vector2(0, 9.8f);

    /// <summary>
    /// Adds an attractor that pulls particles toward a scene node.
    /// </summary>
    /// <param name="id">Attractor identifier</param>
    /// <param name="target">Scene node to attract toward</param>
    /// <param name="strength">Force strength</param>
    /// <param name="sticky">If true, particles can stick permanently</param>
    public void AddAttractor(string id, SceneNode target, float strength, bool sticky = false);

    /// <summary>
    /// Removes an attractor by ID.
    /// </summary>
    public void RemoveAttractor(string id);

    /// <summary>
    /// Adds a sticky zone where particles probabilistically stick.
    /// </summary>
    /// <param name="node">Scene node defining the zone</param>
    /// <param name="stickProbability">Probability (0-1) of sticking on contact</param>
    public void AddStickyZone(SceneNode node, float stickProbability);

    /// <summary>
    /// Sets the physics timestep (default: 1/60s).
    /// </summary>
    public void SetFixedTimestep(float seconds);

    /// <summary>
    /// Advances the physics simulation by delta time.
    /// </summary>
    public void Step(float dt);
}
```

### Timeline Animation
```csharp
namespace SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Maui.Morphysics;

/// <summary>
/// Fluent API for building property animations.
/// </summary>
public sealed class Timeline
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Sets the target scene node for subsequent animations.
    /// </summary>
    public Timeline Target(SceneNode node);

    /// <summary>
    /// Animates a bindable property over duration.
    /// </summary>
    /// <param name="propertyName">Property name (e.g., "X", "Rotation")</param>
    /// <param name="from">Start value</param>
    /// <param name="to">End value</param>
    /// <param name="durationMs">Duration in milliseconds</param>
    /// <param name="easing">Easing function (default: Linear)</param>
    public Timeline Animate(string propertyName, object from, object to, int durationMs, Easing? easing = null);

    /// <summary>
    /// Adds a delay before the next animation.
    /// </summary>
    public Timeline Then(int delayMs);

    /// <summary>
    /// Executes a callback at this point in the timeline.
    /// </summary>
    public Timeline Call(Action callback);

    /// <summary>
    /// Plays this timeline (non-blocking).
    /// </summary>
    public void Play();

    /// <summary>
    /// Stops this timeline.
    /// </summary>
    public void Stop();
}

/// <summary>
/// Collection of timelines attached to the canvas.
/// </summary>
public sealed class AnimationTimelineCollection : List<Timeline>
{
    // Can manage multiple concurrent timelines
}
```

## 🚦 CONTINUOUS VALIDATION STRATEGY

### Self-Checking Mechanism
After implementing each phase, run this validation:

```csharp
public class ImplementationValidator
{
    public ValidationResult ValidatePhase(int phase)
    {
        var result = new ValidationResult { Phase = phase };
        
        switch (phase)
        {
            case 1: // Foundation
                result.Checks.Add(CheckApiCompiles());
                result.Checks.Add(CheckCanvasRenders());
                break;
            case 2: // Morphing
                result.Checks.Add(CheckMorphingWorks());
                result.Checks.Add(CheckMorphPerformance());
                break;
            case 3: // Physics
                result.Checks.Add(CheckPhysicsDeterminism());
                result.Checks.Add(CheckCollisions());
                break;
            // ... other phases
        }
        
        return result;
    }
    
    private CheckResult CheckApiCompiles()
    {
        try
        {
            var canvas = new AnimatedCanvasView();
            var node = new VectorNode();
            canvas.Root = node;
            return CheckResult.Pass("API compiles");
        }
        catch (Exception ex)
        {
            return CheckResult.Fail($"API compilation failed: {ex.Message}");
        }
    }
}
```

## 🛠️ TROUBLESHOOTING GUIDE

### Issue: Canvas not rendering
**Checklist:**
- [ ] SKGLView has non-zero size (check XAML layout)
- [ ] Root node is not null
- [ ] PaintSurface event is firing
- [ ] GRContext is created successfully
- [ ] Enable GPU rendering in project settings

### Issue: Physics not deterministic
**Checklist:**
- [ ] SetDeterministicSeed() called before simulation
- [ ] Using seeded System.Random (not Random.Shared)
- [ ] No DateTime.Now or time-based randomness
- [ ] Fixed timestep being used consistently
- [ ] No floating-point precision differences across runs

### Issue: Morphing looks janky
**Checklist:**
- [ ] Path point counts aligned correctly
- [ ] Easing function applied properly
- [ ] Rendering at 60 FPS (not dropping frames)
- [ ] Path winding direction consistent (CW vs CCW)
- [ ] No NaN values in interpolated points

### Issue: Appium test flaky
**Checklist:**
- [ ] Waiting for CanvasReady event before interacting
- [ ] Using explicit waits (not fixed delays)
- [ ] Screenshots captured after animations complete
- [ ] Deterministic seed set for consistent replay
- [ ] No race conditions in test setup

### Issue: Low FPS / Performance
**Checklist:**
- [ ] Profiled with dotTrace (find hot spots)
- [ ] Object pooling enabled for particles
- [ ] Sprite batching working (one draw call)
- [ ] No LINQ in per-frame code
- [ ] SKPaint objects cached (not recreated)
- [ ] Collision detection using spatial partitioning
